results.rendered_html: of a serpent-king (one of the three chief kings of the Nāgas, the other two being Śeṣa and Takṣaka; the gods and demons used the serpent Vāsuki as a rope for twisting round the mountain Mandara when they churned the ocean,  RTL. 108, 233 ),  MBh. ;  R.  &c.
